title: PostgreSQL — Default Creds → COPY PROGRAM RCE
body:
PostgreSQL with COPY ... PROGRAM is direct RCE as the postgres OS user — if you're a superuser. The default 'postgres' account is frequently superuser and frequently has weak/default creds (postgres:postgres, postgres:<blank>, postgres:password).

PORT GOTCHA — don't assume 5432. It runs on whatever port the admin chose; real boxes use 5433, 5437, etc. Pull the port from your scan and pass it with -p. A default-port assumption misses the service entirely.

THE FLOW:
  1. Connect with the right -p and default creds.
  2. \du — confirm you're Superuser. No superuser = no COPY PROGRAM (fall back to file-read / loot).
  3. COPY a table FROM PROGRAM 'cmd' → RCE. Test with 'id' before firing a shell.
  4. Reverse shell → you land as the 'postgres' user → pivot to Linux privesc.

RCE here is a FOOTHOLD as a low-priv service account, not root. The real chain is postgres-user shell → sudo -l / SUID / GTFOBins → root (this is exactly the Nibbles path: postgres RCE → SUID find → GTFOBins → root).
warn: QUOTING GOTCHAS that waste 20 minutes: (1) COPY ... FROM PROGRAM takes NO parentheses — 'COPY t FROM PROGRAM (...)' is a syntax error; the command is a single quoted string. (2) Use STRAIGHT ASCII quotes — pasting from a browser/doc gives curly ' ' which the parser rejects. (3) To put a literal single-quote INSIDE the program string, double it ('') — that's how the python payload above nests quotes. (4) 'exit code 1, no callback' = the command RAN but the shell couldn't connect: check $lhost is your real tun0 and the port matches your listener (a 444-vs-4444 typo will do it).

choices.label: COPY PROGRAM gave a shell (you're 'postgres' user)
SUID binary found → GTFOBins to root
Found creds/hashes in the DB — hunt reuse
